extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco obliterated the field by 43 seconds over ROGLIČ Primož in a 70km opening ITT, taking 20 seconds at the first check and extending on the final 2km climb to Ortona.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL Remco gained half his winning margin before the final climb, indicating flat power dominance not just climbing strength.
ROGLIČ Primož lost 43 seconds despite riding competently, suggesting UAE's VINE and MCNULTY provide better ITT benchmarking than Jumbo depth.
GEOGHEGAN HART Tao climbed significantly faster than early starters despite slower flat speed, revealing climb-weighted pacing strategy works on mixed ITTs.
Wind-assisted coastal section produced 53kph averages for contenders, meaning first time check separated pure diesel engines from explosive climbers.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 20-second gap at first check indicates current TT form untouchable even before climbing advantage kicks in
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: 43-second deficit on opening day suggests GC deficit management starts immediately, unlike typical Grand Tour buildup
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: 29-second loss to EVENEPOEL while beating specialists KÜNG and nearly matching GANNA reveals underrated TT progression
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: beat MCNULTY despite recent knee injury, suggesting UAE TT depth allows aggressive support tactics throughout three weeks
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: third place despite specialist status indicates current form insufficient to match EVENEPOEL on mixed terrain ITTs
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: broke 22-minute barrier via superior climbing after slow flat splits, validating climber-paced ITT strategy on mixed parcours
